subject: Public Health
title: Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorders
description: A quick, practical guide to Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorders.

hook.text: Alcohol crosses the placenta freely—a developing brain bathed in alcohol can be permanently damaged before birth.

buildup.text: Fetal alcohol spectrum disorders cause intellectual disability, facial abnormalities, and behavioral problems for life.

discovery.text: FASD affects an estimated 1 in 13 children whose mothers drank during pregnancy—far more common than once believed.

twist.text: There is no known safe amount of alcohol during pregnancy—even small amounts may cause harm.

climax.text: Prevention requires education, screening, and supporting women to avoid alcohol during pregnancy—not blame.

punchline.text: No drink is worth the risk to an unborn brain. Zero is the only safe number.

quiz.question: What is the estimated prevalence of FASD among children exposed to alcohol in utero?
